It had the atmosphere of a budget terminal with insufficient seating, noise and unorganized boarding. It was off peak time at Doha airport with plenty of airbridges available! Long wait on the bus with open doors at 40 degrees. Short flight to Dubai on time on a wonderful and comfortable A350-1000.
One of the best airlines I ever flew with. The quality of the service and the plane is over the top, and they still managed to be less expensive that other companies! I love them.

Goa (GOX) to Doha (DOH) in Business Class on B787-8. This sector features Qatar's non-Qsuite traditional layout. While not as compelling as Qsuite, it is nevertheless very comfortable. It is a short hop from Goa to Qatar, just under 3 hours, and everything about Qatar Airways was flawless.

Probably one of the first A380s received by Qatar Airways and the age showed. That said the cabin staff were fantastic expressive and attentive, the IFE was superb and the selection was wide though I would suggest a search option. WI-FI wasn’t working for me Qatar Airways need to refurbish their A380s and not wait on Boeing the 777-9s that are a long way off.

Review Title: a pleasure to be on board
Review: Flew Melbourne to Athens via Doha and return July/August 2024. Check in was smooth. Aircraft very clean. Qsuites are amazing. Crew were very professional and friendly, and looked after us extremely well but not intrusive. Once you order your meals these are given to you on your request as it’s meals on demand. Nothing was too much trouble every request was catered to without issue. If you wanted to sleep they would let you sleep, once awake the crew would then offer your meal. Hot towels presented before and after your meal. Toilets were pristine and were cleaned after each person. The PJs were are nice touch on a long haul flight as was the amenity kit. It was one of the best long haul flights and a pleasure to be on board all 4 flights taken. Thank you Qatar Airways we will definitely fly with this company again.

I am dissatisfied with the service from Qatar Airways following my flight on August 2nd. Despite being told that email is the best way to communicate, my emails have been ignored. I only receive responses after submitting new complaints. This shows a lack of commitment. Qatar Airways has put much effort to the service during the flight which is almost perfect, but my experience of sending emails has shown how indifferent and frustrating the airport team can be.

First experience of Qatar Airways and was very impressed. The cabin staff were amazing. They were extremely friendly and attentive, and always available when needed. The service given was the best I have experienced of any of the major carriers. Will definitely fly with Qatar Airways again, and would recommend others to experience them.

Review Title: help us find our missing luggages
Review: First time flying with this airline and they missed to load our 5 luggages. Now it went missing for 8 days now. We reported the problem from the time we arrived at Dubai airport and we were constantly following up the issue through call and email but no one cared to help us. It is really frustrating knowing that they are the worlds best airline. Hoping that somebody will step up and help us find our missing luggages.

We had a family booking where we  with 3 children under 12 and all of us are allocated to seats not together; we arrived early to the airport and no one helped; we ended up sitting on 3 different rows. The head of cabin crew lied to me before take off and told me she has sorted this out, but that never happened and her response was robotic and she  asked me to write a complaint.

Review Title: waiting for a response to my complaint
Review: One of the people in the group is 6 feet 4 inches so I booked and paid for three front row seats on the Qatar Airways web site. The payment for just over £240 went through and was confirmed. When I checked in we had been moved to a normal economy seat. The check-in staff at Dubai (not Qatar Airways staff) tried to resolve it but couldn't. I was told to go to the transfer desk at Doha where they would have an answer. The staff there could not care less. The answer was 'there is a family sat there so there is nothing we can do'. Then seemed surprised that I wanted a refund and handed me a card at me with some email addresses on it. We boarded and the seats we should have had were occupied by three other people. No babies, no baby bassinets or any other reason we were moved out of them. The tall person in the group had to sit with one leg in the aisle and one under the middle seat. It was uncomfortable. The seat pitch on the 787 was tight and of course the person in front reclined as soon as the aircraft left the ground. My food was rubbish. I did get a refund but 2 weeks later I am still waiting for a response to my complaint. I am unsure how Qatar presents itself as a 5 star airline.

I recently traveled with Qatar Airways from Manchester to Dubai, with a layover in Doha, and unfortunately, the experience was far from the high standards I expected from a reputed 5-star airline. Check-In issues began at Manchester Airport, where the self-service check-in machines were difficult to use, and the staff provided little assistance. The process was unnecessarily stressful and time-consuming, contrary to the promise of digital technology streamlining the experience. Additionally, the staff at the baggage drop-off point were abrupt, warning us about excess baggage fees despite our luggage being well within the limit. This initial interaction was disappointing. Boarding the flight was another frustrating experience. The Qatar Airways representative insisted that each family member, including my autistic children, hold their own boarding passes and passports. This requirement seemed unnecessary and delayed our boarding process. A more considerate approach would have been appreciated, especially given the circumstances. The in-flight experience from Manchester to Doha was underwhelming, particularly the food quality. While the service at Dubai Airport was commendable, it was not provided by Qatar Airways staff. In contrast, the staff at Doha were abrupt, asking for passports in a manner that even my son found rude. This inconsistency in service quality is not what one would expect from a top-tier airline. Overall, my experience with Qatar Airways did not live up to the hype. The service was inconsistent, and the quality of food and customer interaction left much to be desired. Having flown with budget airlines that provided better service, I find it hard to justify the premium associated with Qatar Airways. It seems that the reputation of being the "best airline in the world" might be more about marketing than service delivery.

A storm in Washington DC Dulles International Airport on 3rd August 2024 caused a major delay of our flight QR 708 to Doha for over four hours. The explanation did not come quickly until the passenger started complaining. The aircraft finally arrived but the conditions in the plane weren't appealing. One of the restrooms in the rear right did not have running water for washing the hands, although the flush toilet had water. Meals were being served without even sanitizers to Economy class passengers, notwithstanding the dirt some people had acquired from the toilets. The worst part begins when I miss my flight connection from Doha to Nairobi. I was shuffled from one desk to another and finally landed in Transfer desk A where after queuing for one and a half hours was issued with an Ethiopian Airline ticket to Addis Ababa and a Kenya Airways ticket from Addis-Ababa to Nairobi. I had a meeting in Nairobi which had started on 5th August and I was getting late. On arrival in Nairobi, my 2 suitcases were missing and I reported to KQ and was issued with a reference number NBO/KQ69813. As I write this review on 8th August, no information has reached me on the whereabouts of the suitcases. As the primary Airline I booked, I hold Qatar Airways responsible for the careless mishandling of my luggage as well as the inconvenience and costs of buying new clothes and other items that I need as the long wait continues. It makes me have a very low opinion of the Airline. The was just too much disorganization. You trust Qatar Airways at your own risk, wait, you will be disappointed. It can be disorganized worse than any struggling airline, it is hyped and overrated unnecessarily.

I recently travelled with Qatar and had a very disappointing experience. Customer service over the phone and via email, was terrible and unprofessional charged me more than what I supposed to pay for a ticket by not mentioning a credit card fee charged only if you book over the phone, unfortunately the the fee was not shown until the payment was made. The lounge provided at Adelaide was extremely low cost. It felt cheap and did not meet the standards one would expect from a business lounge of a premium airline. Additionally, my food on board was a letdown. It was served cold which is unacceptable. Therefore my experience with Qatar Airways was far from satisfactory.

Review Title: Customer service at Doha was very poor
Review: Our flight was 5 hours delayed, so we missed our connecting flight. Only option offered was flight next day from Doha! No possibility to use business class or a partner, unless you pay. Customer service at Doha was very poor, hardly no staff to help the customers. At economy class one staff member, at business class next desk 3 staff but no customers. First transfer desk wasn't open, at the second we were told to back to the first one. One desk for about 100 plus people. As Doha is Qatar Airways home you woud expect it to be more fluent.

It was my first time flying Qatar, we arranged our itinerary around flying in Q Suites on a 4 leg journey. We were excited by the prospect of flying in "the world's best business class", as we are often disappointed when we fly business with other airlines. It started badly when we arrived at the lounge and were refused entry, we were told that the tickets were a promo fare with no lounge access. I argued that we were BA silver member so were entitled to access anyway. We were told that if we were flying in economy we could access the lounge but as we are in a business class promo fare we could not. The first leg was a Q Suite, it's decent but was on par with other products we had flown. The second leg was a planned non Q Suite configuration. I was shocked at the quality of the product, it was like business class a la 1990. The person sat next to me was as close as an economy seat, the bed was really uncomfortable. There was no leisure suit. I would say this was the worst long haul business class I ever flew. 2 days before the return journey, we received an email telling us that the first leg of the journey from Nairobi to Doha had an aircraft change and was the old product again. I really didn't want to fly in that plane so I called Qatar. The call to their call centre was virtually inaudible, lots of background noise, the operatives accent was so strong I could hardly understand them.  We managed to change the flight which involved an eight hour stopover in Doha. In addition I had to pay $300 to change our domestic flights to accommodate this. So we now had both legs is a Q suite, or so I thought. The day after going through all this drama we received another email telling us that the second leg of our journey (the 8 hour leg) had changed aircraft to the old configuration. I kept checking their website right up to the flight and they were still selling this flight (QR149) as a Q Suite product 48 hours after they had changed the aircraft to a non Q Suite plane.

My flight was delayed for 8 hours from San Francisco and my connecting flight was already left for Ahmedabad so I had to stay in Doha for 16 hours for my next flight to Ahmedabad. It was very irritating.
Route: San Francisco to Ahmedabad via Doha

Review: Really poor experience. After a missed connection I was offered an overnight hotel stay . I really didn’t want this so asked to stay overnight in the airport and go on an earlier flight. By 2:30am my only solution was to accept the hotel which was a 30 min bus ride from the airport. I travelled on a very shoddy mini bus with 10 men (I was the only female) at 2:30am to a very old and run down hotel. Checking in took another 30 mins where I refused to sign a document accepting I felt safe in the hotel. I was left with no other option . When I raised a complaint with Qatar once I finally got home they do not care. Not worth it especially if it’s a tight connection in Doha. Customer service on the ground had an attitude and we’re not listening to my concerns.

We flew from Madrid to Doha. Overnight flight. It was perfect. Aircraft was comfortable. Staff were very friendly. Service was great. We had a layover of 4 hours in Doha approx. At 9 am we were ready to board but we were booted out of the plane and asked us to go to customer service. No explanation given. There was a long queue at customer service and it was chaos. It took us over an hour to get in front of the line and see an agent. No one explained why we were there, they just told us that we were going to be in the next plane at 02:20 am next day. They paid for our hotel and gave us meal vouchers and transport to and from the airport. We were also compensated in cash afterwards (once we submitted our claim). My only complaint is the lack of information given to the passengers. No one explains anything. They just tell you what to do and where to go. It was a very stressful situation because of the lack of info. If someone would had explained what was going on and WHY, we could had understood the process and our situation better and felt less stress. Travelling is sometimes stressful and there’s no need to add extra stress to it. Other than that, I think Qatar was great. They just need to stop overselling their flights to avoid these type of situations that are stressful for their passengers.

In the future we will try to avoid this airline due to its policy regarding the weight of cabin luggage. They weighed our cabin luggage and added it to the weight of the carry-on bag; since it exceeded the weight, they forced us to check in the cabin luggage. The man who helped us was inflexible and despite the tremendous disruption that losing our luggage would cause us on a 45-day trip to Africa, there was nothing we could do. This is the first time this has happened to us in the last 10 years on an airline; airlines have not weighed cabin luggage for years. Not being able to carry luggage in the cabin due to a maximum weight of 7-8 kilos is a serious problem. It was impossible for me to check in online in advance, as the App and the website did not work.  En un futuro, lamentablemente, intentaremos evitar esta aerolínea por su política relativa al peso del equipaje de cabina. Nos pesaron el equipaje de cabina y lo sumaron al peso del bolso de mano, como superaba el peso nos obligaron a facturar el equipaje de cabina. El hombre que nos atendió fue inflexible y a pesar del tremendo trastorno que nos supondría la pérdida del equipaje en un viaje a África de 45 días, no hubo nada que hacer. Está es la primera vez que nos pasa en los últimos 10 años en una aerolínea, hace años que las aerolíneas no pesan el equipaje de cabina. No poder llevar equipaje en cabina por un tema de peso máximo de 7-8 kilos es un problema grave, pues todas las maletas de cabina, aunque se lleven poco enseres, siempre superan esta norma. Por otra parte, fue imposible realizar el checkin online con la debida antelación, pues tanto la App como la web no funcionaban correctamente. -

Review Title: give you whatever seats they want
Review: My wife and I purchased seats months in advance of our flights from Raleigh to Kilimanjaro and back. On the day of the flight we were told the seats didn't exist, and given other seats is basically the worst spots on the plane. Then when filing a complaint for a refund they deny it saying they can't refund because they gave us seat with "similar characteristics" as what we bought and the policy excludes refunds for that. We purchased seats together in the window aisle and were given seats in the middle aisle on every flight (3 out of 4) and one of which were not even together. I would not call this "similar characteristics" by any stretch of the imagination. This is a vague term they use to allow them to pre sell seats that don't exist, give you whatever seats they want, then claim you are not entitled to a refund due to similar characteristics.  I have had similar situations but always been refunded promptly with any other airline.
Excellent service from the ground staff to flight attendants. Very professional, caring in-flight service. Terrific business class seating and wonderful food.

Perfect flight with very attentive ground and in-flight staff. The aircraft was clean, the food and in-flight entertainment was terrific. The professional flight attendants did a wonderful job ensuring everything was perfect.

Review Title: I can't even say how grateful I am
Review: I never write reviews, but this time I must do it. I want to thank Qatar Airways, the whole crew. You are the best. I was flying from Beijing to Doha, then from Doha to Yerevan. During my 1st flight, I got horrible pain, because of my kidney stone. I don't know how could I deal with it without the crew. I know it might be just part of their job, but the way they treated me, the support I got from them, meant a lot to me. I can't even say how grateful I am. Thank you again and again. Qatar airways is the best for a reason. From now on I know which airline I'm gonna use without a doubt and gonna recommend everyone.

Doha (DOH) to Goa (GOX) in Business Class on 787-8. It was a short flight of just over 3 hours. Seating was Qatar's traditional Business Class (not Qsuite), very comfortable. Service onboard was expectedly world class. There is a reason Qatar is currently the world's best airline. If there is a criticism to be made it was that the WiFi on this flight was extremely slow and spotty.

Copenhagen (CPH) to Doha (DOH) in Business Class on 787-9. The Business Class seating on this aircraft was not the Qsuite configuration. But Qatar's traditional B-Class seats are nothing to complain about. They provide a high level of comfort. Service on board was exceptional, true to Qatar's recognized standards. If there is one area where Qatar falls short it is in the food department. The Indian vegetarian preparation was oily and prepared without attention to detail.

Review Title: overall it was a good flight
Review: The check in process was smooth and simple, they had self check in too which I liked, within 45 minutes I was sitting at the gate waiting to board my 10.45pm flight to Doha. When I arrived on the aircraft I was very kindly greeted by the cabin crew, it was lovely and bright when I got into the aircraft. When I arrived at my seat there was a pillow, blanket, headphones, and amenity kit waiting for me, they also had an adjustable head rest and reading light. Very good variety of entertainment on there and I was very impressed with how attentive the touchscreen was. About 1 hour after take-off they served the meal, it was very good food, then for about 7 hours they let you rest and then another meal - for me it was dinner and breakfast given the time of the flight. Qatar Airways food is not too bad at all! It was a smooth take-off and landing, overall it was a good flight, better than I expected and can't wait to fly home from holiday with Qatar Airways.

The worst possible check in Singapore. We were a family of three flying to Washington DC on 28th May in business class. The check in staff was uninterested, unhelpful almost to the point of being rude. She was taking almost 10 mins per passenger at check in. Onboard service was fantastic and second to none; the true Qatar service I had seen on a previous flight I had taken, but after this incident at Singapore airport, I would never fly Qatar Airways out of Singapore. It’s too stressful.

The journey started from a bus gate to a remote stand via a packed bus. The Boeing 787 was old and needs a new interior in Economy class. My seat was hard with little leg room. The food was not good and no snack before landing; The stewardess was friendly but very slow. I was disappointed.
Ms Santra was extremely helpful in resolving the change in flight and timing. She worked on phone to work with me on a short notice and offered me the flights that were originally anticipated.

I had a deeply disappointing experience with Qatar Airways. My return flight to Houston was canceled without any prior notification or apology from Qatar Airways. I discovered this only when I arrived at the airport, expecting to board my scheduled flight. The complete absence of communication was unacceptable and added unnecessary stress. When I called to check on alternative arrangements, I was informed that the next available flight was four days away due to all flights being fully booked. Qatar Airways made no effort to prioritize passengers whose flights had been canceled, nor could they provide me with the same seat I had initially booked. This lack of consideration and poor management of flight schedules was extremely frustrating and unprofessional. As a result, I am stuck in Doha for three additional nights, incurring expenses well beyond my budget. Despite explaining my situation repeatedly to the office manager, she offered no apology or compensation. There was no reimbursement for my troubles or the extra costs I incurred, and no one took accountability for this total fiasco. This experience has made it clear to me that Qatar Airways has no respect for their customers. The lack of communication, inadequate support, and unprofessional behavior of their staff were inexcusable. My flight is now scheduled for tomorrow morning and they made sure that I realise it’s not even guaranteed.

At the time of my last booking I had a Gold Privilege membership and I did the booking for my travels later this year. I selected my economy seats. Qatar Airways upgraded a part of my travel to business class. Recently, they changed my membership to Silver and they kept my business class upgrade, however, cancelled other seat selections. There was no email, no phone call - I found out about the cancellation by chance. When I contacted Qatar airways, they told me to choose my seats again and pay for my seat selection.

I travelled from Islamabad to Newcastle (UK) on 1st July. My flight from Doha to Heathrow was one of the best I have ever flown. This was mainly due to the cabin crew and their exceptional service. The senior purser and my assigned crew both went out of their way to make sure I was well looked after. Very polite and friendly demonstrating their high professionalism. I have flown business class so many times before but this experience was exceptional. No wonder Qatar is rated the best airline. Although the aircraft (Boeing 777-300ER) was little dated, the service provided was incomparable to the one I received on a latest B 787 I flew on with another airline. The staff I dealt with deserve an extra recognition of course.

I am flying around the world for more than 17 years. I have been silver and gold member with Lufthansa and KLM and just recently I have become silver member with Qatar Airways. I can clearly see different with approach from crew to me as a frequent flyer. They approach you by your name they informed you in advance regarding menu onboard. They ask many times if all is ok or if you require anything to make your trip better. This flight was long for me because 11hr straight so this type of service was really appreciated.  I am not surprised this is best airline in the world. I would like to thank all personnel onboard flight QR901 on 2 July for excellent personal attitude and professionalism. Good Job to all of You. See You soon onboard Qatar Airways

I thoroughly enjoy flying with Qatar airways. From the positive attitude of the ground staff, to the welcoming smiles of the on board cabin crew, in my opinion, no other airline does it like Qatar Airways. I am privileged enough to have flown in both business and economy class whilst using this airline, and all of the times I have done so, the service has been phenomenal. From the food, to the care from the cabin crew, my experience on board has always been one to remember.

One of earliest A380s of Qatar Airways and age was showing on it with little tears on the leather and not all seat features working. The IFE was excellent and and selection was wide the caviar and tuna tartar were excellent too though breakfast choices were quite poor. The cabin service was perfect I can’t wait to see the new First Class Suite.

Review Title: Qatar Airways is my first option
Review: It was a mixed class. Flying economy with my favourite plane the A380. And Business from Doha to Brussels. We were able to get an upgrade for the second flight. By filling up the remaining seats Qatar is letting you taste of their excellent service in business. It's quite addictive. We got seats 1E and 1F and could enjoy the great food and during a sanitary stop my seat was converted to a bed and we could get a really good sleep during the flight. On the first flight from Bangkok to Doha we were seated in the A380 which is very quiet and the seats are large enough to fit my large legs. I've contacted the helpdesk (by chat/ telephone) numerous times and they react quickly and very friendly. For me Qatar Airways is my first option to look for a next flight.

They are fine and do a job but they aren’t anything special. I wouldn’t rank them in my top 10 airlines. Seat was comfortable, food was terrible. Served a purpose flying out of Vietnam back to Manchester.

Qatar airways decided to charge their most loyal clients more Avios for rewards and upgrades. It increase Avios required for upgrades at departure airports by 50%. You can argue that QR is entitled to do what It wants with its reward loyalty program but it should inform its clients through their website or by email. I was informed after requesting to do the upgrade at their platinum lounge at Doha Hamad Airport. I had to accept the transaction and be charged 50% more because the transaction has taken place, and there was no way to revoke it. I will dispute that. Upgrades are unchanged if made online. However, QR blocks online availabilities even if so many business class seats are still unsold 12 hours before the flight. I did try online until the night before but the upgrades were not going through. Although there were 9 business class seats available up to 10 hours before departure. This new policy started on 9th June and is another demonstration that QR with all its frills seems immature, unnautical, and unprofessional. I am disappointed. Because I go out of my way to fly QR as I am happy with their service. This is not just about the devaluation of its Avios currency. . It's their lack of transparency that seems a pattern imbedded in the company culture. I will be accessing if I will continue to do business with QR.

Online Check in and meal preview via app not working. Checkin and security check efficient as usual in Munich. Munich World contact lounge has improved and is pretty good now. The boarding gate had no holding area, it was just a long corridor with about 30 seats and the other 200 pax had to stand. Flight was on time. The big letdown was the poor standard of food. My main course was poor in presentation and taste. The quality of catering had dropped tremendously since they started to serve Doha prepared food for both their out and inbound flights to Munich.

Had a very good flight with Qatar Airways. The cabin manager, Khalid, was superb in his service. He gave true and excellent service throughout the flight. Overall smooth flight from Singapore to Doha, had my 10 year old daughter with me. Only setback ,no ice cream on board.

Having flown Qatar previously I chose to book flights for our holiday to Thailand. I paid extra for seat allocation. On not one of the 3 flights were the seats I paid for allocated to me. The aircraft in each case had not been changed. On one flight the representative at the gate showed me the cabin plan which indicated that the seats I had paid for had been resold to other passengers.  I emailed Qatar with a request for a refund. I have sent dozens of emails and have had only one response with my case number. The website says they will respond in 60 days. This period has elapsed long ago and still no response. Customer service is extraordinarily poor. The flights were very poor, some of the worst service and food I have experienced on a long haul flight.

Review Title: always a pleasure to fly with Qatar
Review: It is always a pleasure to fly with Qatar Airways. I testimonied the professionalism, the dedication and the friendliness of the crew on both flights, and this makes the flight very pleasant. The seat is very comfortable and the individual space is large. Food and drinks are excellent. Fasten seat belt announcements appear on the screen, so your program on the screen is not interrupted and/or you do not wake up when you are sleeping. My experience is the best from all airlines I flew so far. I flew with my 11 year old son - treated with respect like an adult; some gadget toys for kids might be nice.
Route: Hong Kong to Vienna via Doha

Review Title: assume I was not served the right meal
Review: I was not served the correct meal despite my request for western vegetarian on the flight segment from Singapore to Doha. I was shocked that they told me that their reservation record stated that I never requested a special meal request despite my booking itinerary showing that I have reserved a vegetarian meal for all segments. I assume that I was not served the right meal since they did not come back to clarify with me. No service recovery and terrible experience with no sense of accountability or whatsoever.

I am always amazed with Qatar Airways services. The airport, the lounge, the staff, all is excellent. The most important takeaway is it makes me feel secure and little risks. Food is good. Attendants are so friendly and professional. Kudos to great service.

Doha Al Safwa lounge offered great service and food and drinks. Boarding as for most flights I had over the past year via a remote stand from the feared bus gate C41. At first we were told that business would board last (the opposite of my last flight, when I was told that Qatar changed its policy and business class pax would get on the bus first). After half way of boarding, the gate agent decided to hold the economy class boarding and to do the business class boarding now. The boarding procedure via bus gate seems to be unorganized and chaotic. The first thing I was told by the purser on board was that my ordered meal wasn’t on board. I ordered it days before the flight, received a confirmation email and it was even printed on my boarding pass. I was told that the meal booking was in the system, but catering failed to load it. This is the 2nd time this happened to me, the last time for to the non functioning of the app meal booking, which I reported to customer services who decided to ignore my mails. If Qatar offers this service of pre booking meals in business class, they should honor it! The flight itself was uneventful and on time. WiFi did work.

Check in was fast and friendly. Qatar does not permit access to its Bangkok business class lounge to its Platinum members. The contract Miracle lounge does not deserve the name and is one of the worst at BKK airport. Boarding was well organized besides the large number of pax due to the aircraft size. I was lucky to have a seat on the upper deck, which is more quiet. Flight was smooth and uneventful on the magnificent A380. Food was decent, though nothing special. WIFI did not work throughout the flight. Flight arrived on time and disembarkation was fast.

I had a disappointing experience with Qatar Airways on flight QR426. The flight was scheduled to depart at 2:50 PM, but it was delayed until 3:30 PM  This unnecessary delay disrupted my schedule significantly and made me feel that my time was not valued.

Review Title: not able to provide the same food
Review: Experienced worst food service in QR763 from Doha to Montreal. 2 times, for brunch and snack, we were served last and couldn't get what was in their menu. But we got the same reply "we are running out of food". Raised a complaint, but received only generic replies and no solution. Please don't make promises that you are giving world's best dining when you are not able to provide the same food to all of your passengers.

I arrived from Warsaw to Doha 12:06 and the airplane door opened 12:10, the bus took so long to get us to a gate that we only arrived at 12:30 and I arrived my gate to Erbil at 12:40 but the 12.55 flight was left.  It took me until 1:45 with transfer desk to call gate supervisor just to give me another ticket. And he told me no hotel because my connection was 50 minutes and not 45 minutes. He told me to get back at 6 am to see weather I am eligible to accommodation, went back to transfer desk only to find the hotel is fully booked after 4:30.  I messaged Qatar airways and they said that they cant do anything for me as its airport related.

Seat 47H should not exist. Located almost at the rear where the aisle becomes narrower and narrower. Measured from the edge of seatback of 47F aisle seat to the edge of my seat 47H armrest = 20inches. 20 inches of aisle for pax and crew. Meanwhile big pax on right of the same row keeps elbowing me whenever he moves I highlighted this to the inflight supervisor and the pax seated on my right (middle seat) was offered a seat change as he too revealed that he felt that his seat was narrower than usual. Noticed 47C on this flight was not occupied. Anyone seated there would have experienced the same. Row 48 was a 2-3-2 configuration. AMS-DOH myIFE not working.

Most unhelpful airline. Flying with a newborn who has been very sick. Would not help to book bassinet and trying to charge $2.7k to change flights to week later. Communication is terrible and no compassion.
Flight was fine but they lost my luggage. They still tell me they will get back to me soon, while I have been waiting for a response for 8 months.
Route: Amsterdam to Ho Chi Minh City via Doha

Review: I can highly recommend Qatar Airways after flying with them more than once on very long flights, experiencing no problems. The staff are helpful and friendly, both ground and air staff - great service. In-flight experience was excellent. Very nice food and drinks menu and service was excellent. On-board entertainment choice was also excellent.

A solid flight. Excellent food, entertainment, and drinks. The seats are very comfortable. Early departure and arrival. They provide excellent service onboard and cabin crew are really attentive towards passengers needs.

Review Title: 1 hour stuck inside a very hot plane
Review: Connection from Doha to Warsaw on 16th May delayed from 8:35 to 13:12 including an excruciating 1 hour (after boarding) stuck inside a very hot plane in Doha. Landed in Warsaw over 4.5 hours later than the scheduled arrival time. Same flight on the previous day was also delayed by over 3 hours, so not a one-off. Awful experience. Definitely not a 5 Star airline in my view.

Review Title: lost my 7-year-old daughter's luggage and golf clubs
Review: Qatar Airways lost my 7-year-old daughter's luggage and golf clubs. As a result, she was unable to compete in the European Golf Championship, an event she trained eight months for it. Luggage delays happen, but Qatar Airways' handling of the situation was appalling and lacked any compassion. Initially, they seemed unwilling to help. However, after we reported the missing bags, there was a chance they could still arrive in time for the tournament. Qatar Airways informed us they wouldn't transfer the luggage to another airline, even though it could have arrived in time for the tournament. This forced my daughter to withdraw, which was incredibly disappointing, especially after they initially indicated it might be possible to get her clubs there in time. The mishandling of Viviane's luggage, containing irreplaceable custom-made golf clubs and other essential equipment, directly resulted in her inability to compete in the European Golf Championship in Edinburgh. This loss represents not only a significant professional setback for a young athlete with immense promise but also a profound personal disappointment. The lack of care exhibited by Qatar Airways robbed her of a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity, the consequences of which extend far beyond the mere financial value of the lost items. Moreover, the provision of false information by Qatar Airways regarding the location and status of the luggage is a further aggravating factor in this case. The reply to all our emails: Sorry for the inconvenience.

In an effort to keep this within wording allowance I will keep this short. Going to the Philippines was without n event. The problems started on Apr 27th 2024 when we were returning. After arriving at gate in Davao we were told there was a delay but told it was only a few minutes. We were boarded and then offloaded. We were made to site for hours without being told anything. Around 1:30am we were told we would have to wait to 4:30am as airstrip is now closed. At 4:30am we were told we would be rerouted to Cebu instead of Doha. When we arrived in Cebu they were to arrange for a hotel and we would travel by bus.  They only secured 1 bus so it took hours. It was so hot that I started to get leg cramps and had to use a wheelchair. My legs gave me problems from this for days after returning home. Hotel was nice but this time was costly to me as a flight from Miami to Grand Cayman, Cayman Islands had to be rebooked at my expense of US$262 x 2 = US$524. We also lost our hotel reservation in Miami costing us another US$126. I reached out to seek compensation and they basically responded but ignored.

A solid flight with all expectations met. The Plaza Premium lounge in Jakarta is awful but as soon as you board you know you’re on Qatar. Excellent food, entertainment, and drinks. The seats are very comfortable and all the expected amenities are provided. Pyjamas, slippers, large blanket, two pillows and an amenity kit. My only criticism is that after the meal the attendants disappeared, however they answered call buttons immediately. Early departure and arrival.

Review Title: Food nothing to write home about
Review: Treated myself to an upgrade to Business class via an online offer. I was disappointed with the experience, no distinguishing between business and economy when waiting for the aircraft and boarding. Friendly staff and very welcoming. Food was nothing to write home about. Cabin seemed to feel noisy even though it was an early morning flight, couldn't get comfortable for a few hours sleep during the 9 hour flight. Hasn't stopped me booking Business class for my 25 hour return flight in September. Surely lightning cannot strike twice.
I have flown to Doha from Bucharest and all I can say is that is one of the best airlines! They provide excellent service onboard and cabin crew are really attentive towards passengers needs!

The A350-900 and B787s in Qatar Airways are equipped with the herringbone business class suites, not QSuites, The flight was delayed almost an hour and the pilot could not make up much of the time during the flight but we were exhausted and I could not enjoy my meals, my leek soup was watery the Arabic breakfast was good. WIFI was patchy I had a bulkhead seat and one of the cabin staff was gossiping loudly.

A long trip, with the 16.5 hour non-stop from Auckland to Doha, then onwards to Beirut. Both flights were good, with clean and comfortable planes and a consistently high standard of service - though it's always interesting to see how one individual cabin crew can really make the difference. Crew are generally attentive, and always polite. On the flight from Auckland there was a pre-dinner drinks service (a rarity these days), and regular top-ups during the flight. Excellent selection of alcohol. Food was on the small size, and I find they scrimp with the meal and the condiments - no butter, no salt and pepper - but was pretty tasty. I'm not such a fan of Qatar's IFE - whilst there are lots of choices, they all seem quite mainstream and I struggle to find something I find interesting (I also flew Emirates and Air NZ on this trip, and didn't have that problem). Overall a good trip from a consistent carrier.

Shockingly bad ground service. Old aircraft with very outdated seats. Onboard food ok. Onboard service, excellent. Booked for 3 pax. As the result of upgrading one on points (second leg, return) , bookings were detached and two subsidiary pax then offered upgrades on outward leg at half the price offered to me. Change of aircraft on return leg resulted in downgrade from QSuites to 2-2-2 old business class. Seat allocations were not honored on return second leg. Luggage was mishandled in Doha resulting in non arrival. Doha transit staff were rude to young girl in front of us who was denied boarding because she was late to gate. Our luggage was at gate (could hear radio messages) but they never informed us that they weren't loading it. We were permitted to board. On finally getting our luggage that night delivery service person was rude and aggressive and tried to get me to sign waiver in the dark. When I tried to detach attached letter to customer he pulled it off me.  Only saving grace is that service attitude onboard was excellent. QATAR do not deserve any awards until they have a consistent product.

With turbulence very much in the news, the second sector of my flight from Doha was concerning as it was supposed to fly over Myanmar. The captain instead chose to fly around the country but there was still considerable clear air turbulence and breakfast service was cancelled. Not that I was aware as I managed to sleep through all of it! The flight attendants were excellent, passing through the cabin thereafter offering coffees and teas in styrofoam cups and I managed also to get a croissant. Speaking to fellow passengers after we landed, all agreed that QR had reacted very positively in what had obviously been difficult circumstances. As always an excellent airline.

At Dublin Airport we were assisted by an extremely helpful staff member named Cedric. Continued on our journey through Doha and finally on to Perth. In-flight experience was excellent. Very nice food and drinks menu and service was excellent. On-board entertainment choice was also excellent. This was our 2nd time travelling to Australia with Qatar Airways having used other airlines on 3 previous occasions and I will definitely choose Qatar Airways again for my next visit

Nothing to fault, on what were two pretty full sectors. The return leg some weeks later involved a 17.5 hour sector from AKL to DOH, but this did not feel too long. I would recommend them for travel between the UK and Australasia, if the departure & arrival times work (the sector into AKL arrives around 0400). The IFE is reasonable; EK's is better, with much more coverage.

My family and I were extremely disappointed with Qatar Airways. Our recent experience on a cherry blossom trip to Japan was nothing short of a nightmare. We missed our connection in Doha, and the staff suggested a nine to ten-hour journey through Saudi Arabia to Dubai instead of a direct one-hour flight. This was in the middle of the night with young kids. The staff at the airport were unhelpful and unprofessional, and we had to call the police to intervene so we could leave the airport and rest. Despite the long wait, we received no assistance or even a glass of water. Our luggage was lost, and we had to spend a significant amount of money in Doha to get by. The aircraft were cramped and uncomfortable, and despite knowing the flight would be late, no proper arrangements were made. The customer service response to our complaints was dismissive and unsatisfactory. Qatar Airways' treatment of passengers is unacceptable, especially for a five-star airline. Even low-cost carriers have treated us better. We will never travel with Qatar Airways again.

The entire purpose of flying a longer distance for me was to experience the Q suites. Just a few hours before departure I received an email informing me the aircraft will not have Qsuites and it was too late to make any changes. The flight was delayed almost 2 hours. A lesson well learned.

I can highly recommend Qatar Airways after flying with them more than once on very long flights, experiencing no problems. The staff are helpful and friendly, both ground and air staff - great service! The in-flight entertainment was good, so was the quality of everything we were given. There was plenty to eat and drink. Although the food was a little boring sometimes, there was a very good choice of drinks. The flights were on time. I was pleased with my experience with Qatar Airways.
Route: Auckland to Johannesburg via Port Elizabeth

Review: After a disastrous trip on the same route with Turkish Airlines I tried Qatar Airways, and it was like chalk and cheese. All the QR flights were bang on time, and were met by Ground Attendants eager to help. The Cabin Crew were working the whole flight with smiles and good humour. Cannot fault the service and Turkish could learn a lot from Qatar. Totally Professional.
Route: Birmingham to Ho Chi Minh City via Doha

Review: I wasn't allowed to bring my carry-on suitcase into the cabin and I had to check it in. When my case broke, I had to go through extensive documentation repeatedly, and eventually, I gave up trying to follow up with them. The staff was unfriendly and rude to me.

I bought a ticket from Vancouver to Tehran with 2 stops in Seattle and Doha, the first flight was Alaska Airlines to get to Seattle, there was a delay and I got to the gate 10 minutes before the departure, they already closed the gate. We asked for the supervisor and they said they were all supervisors, zero empathy and customer service, kept repeating that it’s customers’ responsibility to take care of their accommodation till find another flight, they even didn’t get anyone’s information.
Route: Vancouver to Tehran via Seattle and Doha

Review: While the prices of the tickets have increased, the quality of service drops e.g. late in serving my meal, no attention to my request, amenities given in a cardboard box. They added a very complex pricing policy and in Business class you can't access to the Business Lounge except if you pay full fare.

I travelled on Qatar Airways from Seattle to Jeddah (4 legs, 3 on economy class and 1 on the first class). The first class experience was amazing. You can book an economy and then look for deals to upgrade to business first. They really went above and beyond and the whole experience was totally different. The onboarding and in-flight economy experience were also better compared to my experience on couple other airlines. The staff is very accommodating. Earning Qatar Airways Avios is a plus.

Flew out on 10 Apr 2024 from Melbourne, via Doha and Dublin for 2 stopovers. Very satisfied with the services from MEL-DOH and DOH-DUB trips. Very good service onboard with the meals and IFE, no major delays with the flight times and the cost of the ticket. Only small gripe was boarding for the DOH-DUB leg, in Hamad airport, as there was a bit of waiting and we had to take a bus to the other side of the runway to board the plane. No problems with luggage, or the transit stopovers. Highly recommend.

I traveled from London Heathrow on 21st April at 15.05, the service was impeccable both ground staff and flight staff, also on onward flight from Doha to Perth I can’t praise the staff enough especially Patricia she was ground staff at Doha I hope I got her name right. I have my right hand/arm in a sling and got so much help so I thank all the staff from checking in at Heathrow to my final destination Perth. Thank you Philomena.
Route: London Heathrow to Perth via Doha

Review: I purchased discounted business class tickets knowing that it did not include lounge access. I am a gold member of Virgin Australia and based on their website had access to partner lounges. I was refused entry to partner lounges in Hong Kong and was let into the lounge in Istanbul but was told that my status does not allow it, but being in business class does. The transit through Doha was the worst I have experienced. Arriving at a bus gate and transiting to a flight at another bus gate took 1.5 hrs . Horrible experience. Only two flights had service that met my expectations. The product was also quite inconsistent 2 aircraft were great and 2 were old and not so great. In regards to the discount business ticket, Virgin Australia confirmed I did have access to partner lounges and Qatar ground staff confirmed I only had access to the gold lounge in Doha and not third party lounges.
An older plane with old business class seats. The service was ok but the Malay Curry had rice not fit to eat. The plane had it’s paint on the inside peeling off  badly upkept in a very bad condition

We flew from Larnaca, Cyprus to Colombo, Sri Lanka via Doha and I was not entirely impressed by Qatar Airways. The checkin (web checkin) was efficient. The plane, especially the A320, felt dated and in need of a good cleaning. The seats are on the small side and somehow really hard and uncomfortable. I ordered a vegan meal option and this was the only thing that really impressed me. The cabin crew was friendly generally and efficient. Slightly better was the legs on the B787, however, for it being one of the newest planes, they also seemed beaten up already. The trim of the inside plastic was not secure at some spots, my screen (I was at an emergency row) had one corner bashed in and in Doha we were assigned to a bus gate with the usual 20 minute trip to the terminal building. Again, the seats were small and felt like an old bus seat rather than an aircraft seat. I wouldn't avoid the airline in the future but I would look if there are any alternative options before booking with it again.

Our original booking which was with a stopover in JNB was cancelled and just our group of 7 passengers were put on a flight that was originally going to Maputo which was diverted to Durban to drop us off and then continue to it’s original destination, only Qatar Airways would go to these lengths! I must thank them! As for the service, it was fantastic, much better than I expected and WIFI was operational. Arrival in Durban was smooth, the asparagus soup was great. All in all the airline is keeping it’s great standards if not improving them.

Review Title: did not entitle me to lounge access
Review: I was contacted in the afternoon of my outbound flight and told they had overbooked business class, and I was invited to change. I explained that I couldn't and was told that was fine. I turned up at Sydney airport that evening and was told I didn't have a seat and couldn't fly.  I was offered an upgrade if I agreed to fly a day later. I had no choice but to accept. At this point, the offer was downgraded to only one of the flights to the UK being upgraded and I was forced to sign a disclaimer waiving any right to further recourse. When I finally boarded my first class leg the following day, there was no power on the aircraft so I was left with a dead and unchargeable device after 2 hours. They tried to charge it in the flight deck for 5.5 hours and it returned with 25% charge. The wifi I paid for didn't work and the in-flight entertainment was an out of date selection and the console only worked intermittently. On my return to Sydney, I returned via Gatwick and was forced to open my suitcases and abandon some of my luggage and they explained that this was because no case could be over 32kilos. I headed to find the lounge and eventually found their shared lounge and was told that my ticket did not entitle me to access.  When I was finally aboard and the flight manager invited me to share feedback, I politely explained that I'd had a less than ideal experience with Qatar so far and a challenging morning. She smiled and assured me that the lounges in Doha were lovely. This proved to be academic advice as I was declined access there too. At this point, exhausted and less than half way through a 28 hour journey, I asked to speak to the Qatar Airlines Supervisor. This man waived a piece of laminated paper at me and explained, as if this were common knowledge, that Qatar offered 4 types of business class ticket and that I had purchased a 'business class lite' ticket. Furthermore, if I'd read the small print on their website I would see that it did not entitle me to lounge access.
The cabin is clean. Flight attendants are polite. Seats are comfortable and can be reclined easily. The food is delicious and fresh. Flights departed on time.

I'm a loyal Qatar Airways customer and absolutely love their service, especially the fantastic flight attendants. The only downside is the long wait for buses at Doha Airport during connecting flights. It would be great if they could find a solution to streamline this process and save passengers time and discomfort.

Checkin fast and friendly, security and automated passport check done within 10 minutes. Frankfurt lounge basic and nothing to shout about. Boarding well organized and departure on time. Before departure my favorite rose champagne served, which was out of stock after take off, the same happened a week earlier on my flight from Doha to Frankfurt. Food was ok, service very good. Qsuite provides a lot of privacy, but seat too narrow for a decent sleep. Arrival in Doha 15 minutes ahead of time at a proper contact gate. Overall a good flight; except for some catering shortcomings.

Review Title: did make up for our disappointment
Review: Chose the airline due to them operating the Q Suite on this route and paid £260 for us to sit together. Sadly no QSuite and average service. The ground crew and lounges at DOH did make up for our disappointment.

Travel with Qatar Airways is an enjoyable experience. Aircraft always clean, kindness of cabin crews and for the quality of food served both at aircraft and lounges. The only problem is the time required to do the transfer at Doha airport. From the landing to the security control it use to take one hour and if you have for example a connection time of 90 minutes you have to run, losing the opportunity to enjoy the offer of service and shop at the airport, but above all, you risk losing the connection.

Review Title: compensate us for half of the bags value
Review: Reschedule of outbound flights was quite inconvenient for us. Had to contact customer service to change them. At least, they did a good job. Flights delayed between one and two hours. Quite mess in the flight Doha-Saigon due to miss of buses from terminal to aircraft during the boarding process.  Two bags broken. After inspection at the arrival, they took nearly one month and a half to set that they will compensate us for half of the bags value. Transfer still pending to be processed. The case needed a follow up from my side after one month, because no action taken.
Route: Amsterdam to Phnom Penh via Doha

Review Title: not enough food for the 6h flight
Review: Check in, security check and boarding fast and efficient as always in. Last minute aircraft change from a B789 to an older 788, which caused a lot of unhappiness among those who paid for their bulkhead seat, and were assigned a random seat. But the professional purser defused a tense situation. Flight itself was uneventful, service slow and not enough food for the 6h flight. Scrapping the sandwich service before arrival is not a good idea. Catering ex Munich used to be very good, but the food was disappointing in taste and presentation.

A good plane and crew on this leg. Had a middle seat assigned but a window seat was free and they moved me there. Crew was excellent. Food and beverages good. Watched movies ate too much and slept, so a great flight.

What a disappointment this flight was. It was supposed t0 be an A350 in Q suites but substituted with an 11 year old A330 in a 2-2-2. The seats were narrow and space limited so much that the lady by the window moved and I had a pair to myself. Crew were great and the rest of the product was good.

Review Title: happy to fly with Qatar again
Review: Our first time flying with Qatar. We booked direct with the airline which hassle free. Flight times were good for a long haul flight and the price was competitive. Communication from the airline was good throughout. We checked in online. Bag drop was very easy and quick. The service on the actual flight was excellent. The plane was clean and appeared to be quite new. Transfer at Doha was easy for our onward flight. I will definitely check out their prices on future flights and would be happy to fly with Qatar again.
Route: Manchester to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

Review: Goa to Doha in Business Class on a Boeing 787-8. Check-in at Goa Dabolim airport was a breeze as was the pre-flight experience. Flight was on time. The short hop to Doha (3 hours) was pleasant. Qatar's hard product & soft product were expectedly outstanding. This is a highly convenient flight for residents of Goa as it obviates the need to fly to Mumbai.

I have always received fantastic service from the Qatar Airways staff both at the airport and in the airplane. This time was no different: simply world-class customer service with smiles, lots of personal attention, careful and correct consideration of the customer's needs and comfort and outstanding politeness overall. I dare to say that QR's cabin crew can serve as an example of good customer service to any other airline. During my trip I used two lounges: one in Helsinki and one in Bangkok. The BKK lounge was a true Qatar-branded lounge and offered a true luxury experience. Great food, great service, beautiful interior design and lots of comfort and peace to prepare for a long flight. Would love to visit again. As for the flights, Qsuite offered a close to perfect business class comfort. The door improves privacy a great deal. The seats even have a massage function and plenty of controls to find you the right settings. I am 190 cm tall and was able to sleep comfortably in the space provided by the Qsuite. The entertainment system worked great and offered a variety of movie options from horror to romcom. Music options even had heavy and extreme metal which I liked as a fan of the genres (I think you could bring even more of these available, QR). During the flights, it was possible to dine however and when you wanted, exactly what you'd want when paying this much extra. Food options were plentiful and delicious with good presentation, and wine and drink selection complimented the food options very well. No complaints here either. Qatar Airways, I want you to keep doing what you are currently doing. Your understanding for what the customer wants is on an excellent level and you are able to provide solutions and service to match the customer expectations.

Review Title: blame each other for the delay
Review: Mumbai to Doha with IndiGo. Both IndiGo & Qatar Airways provide very poor support following a 3 hour delay with no food or ongoing flight support for missed connection. Arrived at London Heathrow 10 hours after scheduled arrival time. Attempted to resolve issue and both airlines blame each other for the delay and missed connection. The only way we became aware of our alternative flights was because we had the Qatar app which was updated after a four hour wait in Doha.

Review Title: Left my mobile phone on flight
Review: Left my mobile phone on flight which landed in Doha. Immediately informed Qatar customer services who made a phone call and told me the phone had been found.  I asked if they could get it to me before my onward flight but the phone had to go through their lost/found "procedures". Completed form at haqiba qatarairways report inquiry, sent endless emails, asked our travel agent to contact them on my behalf, asked my son to contact them on my behalf, both from UK as we were in Japan. Absolutely no response. Fast forward 4 weeks and end of holiday, transiting back through Doha I went to Qatar help desk and explained the situation. The gentleman on the desk made a phone call and informed me that they didn't have the phone. So what happened to it? What happened to the courtesy of replying to my, my agents, my sons phone calls and emails? I know that it was my responsibility to take care of my phone but when you have been told that it has been found and then unfound what am I supposed to think?

Review Title: American Airlines is lowering the standard
Review: I am a big fan of Qatar Airways but since they have partnered with American Airlines the aircraft is changeable. I had to fly from Doha to Philadelphia via and the flight was operated by American Airlines and the service was below average. Air hostesses were least bothered to arrive on ringing them. also they were hesitant to put your overhead luggage or even straighten them out. My point is once we book Qatar Airways we expect standard but the change to American Airlines is lowering the standard. Next time i will not want to travel with American Airlines for 13hr long journey.

As a Silver member of Qatar FF program I was very surprised to be upgraded on this sector flying to Sydney, Australia.  I was therefore surprised to be upgraded from economy to business class on this sector. The check-in person at Manchester was lovely but I couldn't give you her name as she did not have a name tag on, neither did the person who helped me with the auto check-in counter. The QSuite on this 777-ER was exceptional, large space and not confined like other airlines. All the bells and whistles as one would come to expect from the plethora of buttons available that do everything to enhance the flying experience and comfort within your suite. The seat, blanket and pillows were comfortable and warm. The food & beverage service was excellent, the cabin service (by Natalia) was exceptional and the attention to detail was without fault. As the plane was ascending out of Manchester, the door of my suite slid back off its tracking and hit my right shoulder and left knee. Once we were at altitude Natalia and Mary (Cabin Services Manager) were there instantly to assist me by offering ice and towels to aid my shoulder and knee. This was not required as little harm was done. They couldn't have been more helpful or apologetic for something that was outside their control. The flight was smooth, the Captain was informative without being overly so and the staff where fantastic. I couldn't see how Qatar had over 4,500 options for TV, Music and other entertainment and therefore did not use this service. The cabin wifi & connectivity did not appear to work, having said this, I'm not the most literate or tech savvy guest so perhaps in fairness to Qatar, it was available but I just could not connect. On arrival in Doha I headed to the Silver lounge for a shower and this helped my shoulder and knee. Overall, an excellent experience with one minor glitch (door above). Don't know how I got the upgrade (after all these years of flying with Qatar) but it was most welcome and having flown in business class with many other airlines, it was simply the best!

Flight left Manchester about 35 minutes late, Pilot stated the reason was for the late departure was the late boarding of passengers and arrived at Doha 30 minutes late, our flight to Kuala Lumpur was due to depart in 45 minutes, but met at the gate to be told flight had closed and we would be put on the next flight 12 hours later. Qatar Airways put us in a nice hotel and paid for meals etc. It was frustrating as we lost out on a hotel I had paid for for our original arrival, and on arrival in Malaysia had to wait 7 hours in the airport for our flight as opposed to 3 hours we had planned. The Boeing 787 flight was ok, food mediocre except for the roll they offer prior to landing was awful, in contrast the Boeing 777 are showing their age. Seats very uncomfortable, entertainment slow to respond and my touch screen not working. The cabin crew were very attentive and polite on all flights, it was the same for the return flight. I claimed for the delay as per regulations and they sent a long apologetic response within 48 hrs claiming the delay was because of a Air Traffic Issue, which was not true.
Route: Manchester to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

Review: Boarding was well organized; with a proper gate and not the dreaded bus transfer to the aircraft. The newer B789 seat has more privacy with the sliding door, but the footrest is too narrow for tall side sleeper like me. I am flying Qatar often for many years, and today was the first time that that the service was well below average. Maybe it was the full business cabin, maybe the flight attendant had a bad day. It started with the welcome drink, which was served while the aircraft was taxiing already (40 min after I got to my seat), and was taken away 2 min later because the aircraft was ready to take off. The meal service dragged on for almost three hours, while the empty main course plate was sitting on my tray for almost an hour. The beverage of my choice was never served until after the main course was served (I had to call the attendant), and was given another drink, because the drink of my choice was out. When it came to distribute the hot towel after the meal, she did not have enough for the last row where I was seated, and she never bothered to come back to us. There was a light meal before arrival, which I requested for as late as possible, but was told 2h before arrival that I had to eat now, because the galley was going to close. So I had one hour between meals. Wifi worked well throughout the flight, arrival was 20 min ahead of schedule.

Everything was perfect, from the check-in to the arrival. The business Class with the closing door is a game changer which permit to have enough confidentiality to work properly, and the global comfort is clearly one of the Highest I tried. The cabin crew is really nice, always smiling and present when needed.

Checkin, security and immigration was fast and efficient. Contract lounge was comfortable with decent food. Boarding was well organized. The flight was operated by a leased Oman Air A332, with a QR flight number and crew. Food was decent, but not spectacular on this 5h day flight. Service was outstanding, probably also because of just 6 pax in the front cabin. The best part of the flight was the wide and large seat. I slept for a solid 3 hours, something which I can’t, even on longer overnight flights. The seat is so much more comfy than the Qsuite or the herringbone seats on the Qatar fleet. I hope that the upcoming improved Qsuite will be something like this. WIFI not working throughout the flight, there was not even a signal to log on. Overall a very good flight, mainly due to the comfortable seat on a non QR aircraft.

This was my first time flying Qatar in Business class, having flown in their economy class several times. I was not impressed. Boarding at Doha was a mess with a disorganised gate staff who would allow everyone to board at the same time not paying much attention to priority passengers. The crew were very polite and friendly. I was never addressed by name and the crew never introduced themselves or really explained the "dining on demand" concept. I was never asked if I would like to have a light meal before landing. Yes, I know I could have asked but for a so-called 5* airline one would expect a more pro-active service. This was a day flight and the crew didn't really offer me drinks outside the meal service.

Boarding via a bus gate, I am grateful that Qatar finally changed its policy and let Business class pax board first. Flight on time, service great as always in Qatar business class. I tried to pre order my meal through the app, which was not working. 4 emails about the issue to customer services remain unanswered till today. The big letdown on this flight was the catering. The flight departed Doha at 3 am, and got into Colombo at 9.30 am. A big 4 course meal was served after take off at about 4 am, and no breakfast was served upon arrival. I saw hardly anyone eating on this flight. Does Qatar really expect their passenger to have a 4 course meal at 4 am? And why is there not even a light breakfast on arrival, as it is for comparable flights, e.g. Doha to Bangkok? All passengers want to do on a red eye nightlight is to sleep, and eat something before arrival.
Excellent food and flight attendant service. Shanika is an exquisite stewardess. Qatar Airways have become a very good carrier. Would fly with them again.

The flights were steady enough, both legs were a different experience. I have flown with Qatar airlines a few times and I think that they are average. In flight entertainment was different on both flights. My food was average and not very tasty. Cabin crew are friendly. Biggest negative was before taking off from Doha we were sat on the plane for an hour without air con and it was extremely uncomfortable.

First time taking this as recommended by my friends, was sceptical but in the end enjoyed the flight and Doha Airport. Staff keep feeding till you are full. Friendly staff and clean plane. Exciting Airport with lots of shopping and eateries (Asian, Indian, Fusion). Value for money.  Will recommend to others and taking this airline again.
Route: Munich to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

Review Title: just one meal on this flight
Review: There was just one meal on this flight. Service was unorganized and took 3.5h to complete. Food was presented on a tiny tray and was not good in terms of taste and amount. There was no 2nd sandwich service as they used to do on flights to Europe. When I asked the purser why there is no more food, I was told that the Boeing 787 does not have enough storage space, since catering to Europe is done on Doha for the in and inbound flight.

Reserved aisle seats 3 months prior, as I have problems with my hip joint and need to extend the leg. At the time of the checking we were given completely different seats, in the middle, and separated from my friend I was traveling with) . Complete lack of cooperation and understanding from the airport Qatar staff and the person at the check in desk was rude and arrogant to me. The seats in economy class were very uncomfortable and my food was horrible.
Very happy with Qatar Airways, A little bit cramped with space on the first route from Copenhagen to Doha. The crew know what they are doing and the service is good as is the food served

Swift and friendly check in at BKK airport, boarding well organized and smooth. Aircraft ex Cathay Pacific aircraft with more comfortable seats than the Qsuite. Service and catering very good. WiFi not working throughout the flight. Oryx Entertainment system on CX leased aircrafts a watered down version of the usual system with limited Movies and Music choices. Flight on time

The delay of my flight from Haneda to Doha caused a bit of chaos upon arrival. Despite my efforts to reach the gate promptly, I was denied boarding due to luggage issues, and the lack of assistance added to the frustration. I paid for an Economy Comfort package, which was supposed to include an aisle seat. However, upon boarding, I was informed that no aisle seats were available, despite assurances beforehand. Additionally, my luggage tag was lost during the boarding pass exchange, leading to further uncertainty upon arrival in London. What was meant to be a 19-hour journey turned into a 27-hour ordeal. When I requested assistance from staff regarding a re-arrangement they promised to get back to me within 5-10 minutes, but no one followed up until I reached out again. The security queue at Heathrow on departure from the UK was incredibly long, with a wait time of about 1.5 hours just to get through. Overall, while the crew onboard was courteous and provided great service, the experience was marred by these inconveniences.
Route: Tokyo to London Heathrow via Doha

Review: They convinced me that I needed to pay $1500 to add my middle name to my flight, or they wouldn't allow me on the plane. When I got to the airport, they didn't put my middle name on the ticket and I didn't have any issues. I paid $2300 to upgrade our seats to business class from Doha to Montreal. Not even an hour into the flight, the seat reclined on its own, pinching my leg. The seat wouldn't stay up the rest of the 13.5 hour flight.

Review Title: no attempt to address our complaint
Review: I have sent 5 emails and have only received 2 automatic response emails after initiating our complaint 10 weeks ago. I have sent all of the relevant documentation to support our claim for a refund. Our group of 3 booked adjoining Exit Row seats on the Upper Deck of an A380 from Doha to Perth after a connecting flight from Amman. We paid for our Exit Row seats on August 22 2023 and received email confirmation that day. What was a shock to us is that when we were at Amman airport we got our tickets for the second leg, from Doha to Perth, we saw that we had been seats on the lower deck. We said this was wrong and showed our August confirmation for seats 30 A, B and C on the upper deck. We were told by Qatar Airways staff in Amman that it could only be fixed up in Doha. We were also told that the reason was that the plane had been changed from an A380 (which was not correct) . Our connecting flight from Amman to Doha was not only cancelled but deleted altogether meaning we had a 10.5 hour transit instead of the original 2.5 hours. Then at the Boarding Gate in Amman we were informed that there were 2 Exit Row seats available in the Upper Deck and that these had been put on hold for us and to see the Qatar Ground staff in Doha. We were in a queue for almost an hour at the Qatar Service Desk in Doha. I asked how the seats we had booked and paid extra for in August, and provided emails and a hard copy to verify this, were no longer available for us? We were told that it was a system error. There was no apology at all for the system error. We were further disappointed to find out that the 2 Exit Row seats on the Upper Deck that we had been told by staff at the boarding Gate at Amman had been “reserved” were not actually retained for us and that only 1 Exit Row seat was available. Understandably we were not happy that we did not receive the Exit Row seats that we had booked and paid for more than 3 months prior. Through no fault of our own we were victims of a Qatar Airways system error. We did not complain to the flight attendants on the Doha to Perth flight as we understood that they had no control over the seat allocation. However, we politely asked the people in seats 30A and 30B, that we had paid for in August, when they had purchased their seats and were informed that they had done so about 2 weeks prior. I chose Exit Row seats on the Upper Deck because of the extra space and the cabin configuration of 2 by 4 by 2 seats and because my wife and I would be seated next to each other and the other member of our group would be in the next available Exit Row seat. Instead of all being seated next to each other in Exit Row seats we were split up and certainly didn’t get what we paid for. Plus all of the wasted time trying unsuccessfully in Amman and then at the Transit Desk in Doha to sort out the error created by Qatar Airways assigning our seats to someone else. It is bad enough that we didn’t get 2 of the Exit Row seats that we had paid for that were allocated to other people who booked well after us. What is worse is that there has been no attempt by Qatar Airways to address our complaint let alone issue the refund to which we are entitled. Our complaint isn’t complex.

Review Title: I hope they update the A380
Review: We flew on probably the first A380 the airline received but it’s up keep is very good the caviar was excellent and the rack of lamb was perfect the entertainment system though the screens were huge and not very cooperative it is very dated as for WiFi was bad to say the least. The seating in First Class was good and the toilets are huge I hope they update the A380.

Service was ok, pretty good on my aisle and extremely slow where my wife was seated. Layout was good, but not exceptional, I’d rate them maybe 7/10. Landing in Doha, we then had quite a long wait for steps to be brought to the aircraft and then a 10 to 15 minute bus ride to the terminal. There is a lot of competition for routes from Europe to Australasia and think I’ll pick one of the other options in future.

12th February 2024 From check in to get onboard remarkable experience. Since steping at Hamad International airport every one was so welcoming. Check in agent was very professional and helpful. Al mourjan lounge was realy good. Great tasting food. On board all cabin crew was kind professional welcoming. Seat very good, leg space and incredible service, and a very clean plane. Inflight entertainment was great too. The staff was very polite. Arriving at London, Qatar staff gave me Fast track card that helps me to pass imigration quick. Thank you Qatar Airways.
Remarkable experience. Great tasting food, leg space and incredible service, and a very clean plane. Inflight entertainment was great too. The staff was very polite.

My last travel in January 2024 the food quality and presentation had deteriorated and the service. During  booking one has to pay for seats in case one does not want to wait until check-in. On the first leg the crew mechanically did their job. It is ok, but not what you expect from Qatar Airways. Amenity kits are not provided any longer on every flight during the night. Nonetheless you sometimes still find the old welcoming and service oriented Qatar Airways spirit. The crew on the second leg was outstanding as well as ground staff at JRO. Flying Qatar Airways is still a good value for money, but it was not the extraordinary experience.

What a trip, highest level possible, probably. Lounge in Frankfurt a bit underwhelming as Qatar uses Priority Pass Lounge that isn’t up to Qatar‘s standards. From boarding the plane in Frankfurt to Doha, transferring in Doha, visiting their business class lounge there and onward flight from Doha to Melbourne in their QSuite, nothing but praises! Food outstanding, wine list outstanding, seat (and sleep) comfort outstanding: you can’t get it any better!

No complaints about the service or products onboard. However, they lost my bag at JFK for a week, and when I had to waste a day of my own vacation time to go buy some clothes, they refuse to fully reimburse me and I had to chase and jump through hoops to get any compensation offer from them.  It has been few months, and I have not gotten anything yet. All this over a couple hundred dollars; it's utterly disgraceful.
Route: New York to Johannesburg via Doha

Review: When I arrived at my departing airport, Baltimore Washington, my flight was cancelled for my first leg due to bad weather. This part of the flight is with American Airlines.
